name: Edge AI with Phi-4 Models
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements. Edge device hardware costs are one-time CapEx, not included in monthly estimates.

services.name: Azure Container Registry
services.purpose: Model container registry — stores ONNX-optimized Phi-4-mini containers for edge deployment

services.name: Azure IoT Hub
services.purpose: Edge device management — model deployment, configuration, telemetry collection

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Cloud fallback — complex queries escalated from edge when Phi-4-mini confidence is low

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Model artifacts — ONNX weights, quantized models, edge config packages

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: Edge fleet monitoring — model inference latency, accuracy drift, device health

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Edge telemetry aggregation — inference quality, cloud escalation rates, model version tracking

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Device certificates, model signing keys, cloud API credentials

services.name: Azure DevOps / GitHub Actions
services.purpose: ML pipeline — model quantization, ONNX conversion, container build, edge deployment CI/CD

optimization_tips: Run 90%+ of inference on-device with Phi-4-mini — cloud fallback only for low-confidence or complex queries
Use INT4 quantization for Phi-4-mini — 4x smaller model, 2x faster inference, minimal quality loss
Deploy model updates during off-peak hours via IoT Hub — reduces bandwidth cost and device contention
Use IoT Hub Free tier for dev — supports 500 devices and 8K messages/day at zero cost
Cache model containers in ACR close to edge regions — reduces pull latency and bandwidth
Batch edge telemetry uploads — send every 5 minutes instead of per-inference to reduce IoT Hub message costs
